title: Updating Arrays 🔄
description: Modify data stored inside your arrays.

slides.title: Replacing Items 🪄
slides.content:
We know how to get items *out* of an array (`backpack[0]`).

To update an item, we treat `backpack[0]` exactly like a standard variable, and use the `=` sign to put new data in it!

slides.title: Swapping Out 🔄
slides.content: Let's change the first item from a Sword to a Bow.

quizData.question: If `int[] nums = {1, 2, 3};` and we run `nums[1] = 99;`, what does the array hold now?
quizData.explanation: nums[1] points to the SECOND slot (which held 2). It gets replaced with 99.

slides.title: Alternative Initialization 🏗️
slides.content:
Sometimes we know we need 5 items, but we don't know what they are yet.

We can create an array of empty "slots" by using the `new` keyword.
`int[] blankScores = new int[5];`

This creates 5 empty boxes of type `int`. We can fill them later.
